# Token Saver

> Quality-aware token efficiency for AI agents. Diagnose waste, optimize safely, and prove that each successful task costs less.

Canonical repository: https://github.com/SiruGao/token-saver

## Current status

Token Saver is an early-stage OpenClaw skill. It currently provides advisory rules for task-aware model selection, context hygiene, concise responses, repeated-read avoidance, cache-friendly prompt structure, and efficient tool use.

The project does not currently claim to be a universal proxy or production gateway. Planned runtime capabilities are clearly labeled in the roadmap.

## Product direction

Token Saver is being developed as four connected components:

- Token Saver Doctor: local analysis of agent sessions and configuration.
- Token Saver Gateway: safe request optimization with fail-open behavior.
- Proof Ledger: provider usage, cost, retry, latency, and task-outcome accounting.
- Quality Guard: detection of rework and regressions caused by optimization.

Primary metric: cost per successful task.

## Important documents

- README: https://github.com/SiruGao/token-saver/blob/main/README.md

- Benchmark policy: https://github.com/SiruGao/token-saver/blob/main/docs/BENCHMARKS.md
- Contributing: https://github.com/SiruGao/token-saver/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

## Core principles

- Measure before optimizing.
- Prefer deduplication, caching, delta context, and retrieval over lossy summaries.
- Use provider-reported usage as billing truth.
- Include retries and rework in cost calculations.
- Keep processing local by default.
- Fail open on unsupported or unsafe transformations.
- Publish reproducible benchmark evidence for headline claims.

## Target ecosystems

OpenClaw, Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, OpenCode, Hermes Agent, Cursor, Cline, Roo Code, MCP clients, RAG applications, CI/CD agent workflows, and custom LLM applications.

## Search concepts

AI agent token optimization, LLM cost optimization, context engineering, context compression, prompt caching, AI FinOps, token observability, MCP optimization, reversible compression, local-first AI, provider usage reconciliation, cost per successful task.
